title: Understand: Protection of Freedom: Unenumerated Rights
description: Check your understanding of unenumerated rights with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: The Fourteenth Amendment: Citizenship, Freedom, and Equality
Understand: Protection of Freedom: Unenumerated Rights

choices.content: Rights that are not stated in the Constitution
Personal rights that are listed in the Bill of Rights
Fundamental rights that every person has
Federal powers not listed in the Constitution

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is an example of an unenumerated right?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The right to travel freely
The right to vote
The right to trial by jury
The right to bear arms

assessment_items.question_content:
**How has the Supreme Court interpreted the Fourteenth Amendment’s relationship to unenumerated rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Supreme Court has recognized certain unenumerated rights as fundamental and held that states must not violate them.
The Supreme Court has ruled that unenumerated rights are not real, and all rights must be enshrined in the Constitution.
The Supreme Court has acknowledged that unenumerated rights exist but has said that states must pass laws in order for those rights to be protected.
The Supreme Court has not made a ruling on unenumerated rights, allowing lower court rulings to stand.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Supreme Court has held that which part of the Fourteenth Amendment protects unenumerated rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Due Process Clause
The Citizenship Clause
The Equal Protection Clause
The Commerce Clause
The idea that the Due Process Clause protects certain fundamental rights that aren’t specifically listed in the Constitution
The belief that a government’s legitimacy is only justified when it has the consent from the society it governs over
The political system in which the government’s power is restricted by laws or a written constitution
The political principle in which governing happens through representatives elected by the people

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which unenumerated right did the Supreme Court protect in its decision in *Lochner v. New York*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The freedom of contract
The freedom of speech
The right to contraception
The right to a jury trial

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which unenumerated right did the Supreme Court protect in their decision in *Griswold v. Connecticut*? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The right to privacy
The right to keep and bear arms
The freedom to marry
The freedom of contract

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the significance of the Supreme Court’s decision in *Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health Organization* (2022)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It declared that the right to an abortion was not protected under the Constitution.
It protected the right to contract as an unenumerated right.
It incorporated the right to marry and applied it to the states.
It was the first case to establish that there were protected rights not listed in the Constitution.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is an example of an unenumerated right?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The right to a fair trial
The right to legal counsel
The right to petition
The right to marry

assessment_items.question_content:
In *Dobbs v. Jackson,* the Supreme Court’s majority argued that there needed to be a two-part test to determine whether an unenumerated right is worthy of constitutional protection. **What are the TWO parts of that test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The right needs to be listed in the Bill of Rights.
The right needs to be narrowly defined.
The right has a long history and tradition of use.
The right has to protect a specific minority group.

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to many scholars, which amendment is the foundation for the belief that people retain rights not listed in the U.S. Constitution?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The First Amendment
The Fourth Amendment
The Ninth Amendment
The Fifteenth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
**. The Ninth Amendment states that any right not listed in the Bill of Rights is retained by \_\_\_\_. **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the president
the Senate
the states
the people
